opps.title: INTEGRATED PROGRAM MANAGEMENT AND INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY SERVICES
opps.description:
The Coast Guard Accessions Service Center (CG ASC) seeks contractor support to enhance recruiting operations through program management, analytical services, and advanced information technology. A key focus of this requirement is the development, deployment, and sustainment of custom business applications using Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI). The contractor will deliver business process automation solutions, including Robotic Process Automation (RPA), to streamline operations, improve efficiency, and support data-driven decision-making.

Additionally, the contractor will provide graphic design and content creation services to support both internal and external communications. This includes designing user interfaces for applications, developing marketing materials, and producing visually engaging content for outreach and stakeholder engagement. All content must align with Coast Guard branding and approval protocols.

Other services include planning and analysis led by Project Management Professional (PMP) certified personnel, performance management, strategic planning, meeting facilitation, documentation, process improvement, change coordination, reporting, and knowledge management. Personnel must have relevant certifications and government experience.

The period of performance is one base year plus four option years. Work will primarily occur at Coast Guard Headquarters in Washington DC, with possible off-site work as approved. All contractor staff must meet pre-employment screening and background investigation requirements.
win_reasons: Agency: BLN24 has past performance here (relationship + credibility advantage)

opps.title: Continuous Monitoring & Agile Security Services (CMASS) IV
opps.description:
***03/05/2026: Estimated solicitation release date updated to 03/31/2026, estimated period of performance start date updated to 09/16/2026, and anticipated award date updated to 09/01/2026.***
***01/13/2025: Estimated solicitation release date updated to 03/02/2026.***
***10/29/2025: Estimated solicitation release date updated from 11/03/2025 to 01/13/2026; Small business se aside updated from N/A to Full; Small business program updated from SB to 8(a); Anticipated award updated from 3/1/26 to 4/1/26***
***9/24/2025: Estimated solicitation release date updated from 09/11/2025 to 11/03/2025; Dollar Range updated from $10M-$20M to $5M-$10M.***

USCIS has a requirement for Continuous Monitoring & Agile Security Services (CMASS).  USCIS seeks to establish a more sustainable and effective continuous monitoring program, leveraging data from security and governance tools to enhance our ability to identify and mitigate cybersecurity risks, evaluate emerging technologies, streamline security-related tasks. Additionally, we seek to transform the Ongoing Authorization (OA) program to incorporate new capabilities and risk models for Machine Learning (ML) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) and implement appropriate governance measures. The CMASS resources will continue to support security authorization and continuous monitoring activities, the Vulnerability Management & Configuration Management Programs, role-based cybersecurity training, and system stakeholder outreach to ensure USCIS meets security and DHS Information System Performance Plan (ISPP) requirements.

opps.title: Immigration Biometrics and Identity Services (IBIS)
opps.description: The combined USCIS Immigration Biometrics and Identity Services (IBIS) project will deliver secure, scalable, and modern IT solutions supporting data analytics, machine learning, entity resolution, biometrics and identity management, and scheduling for the nationâ€™s immigration system. Leveraging agile methodologies and advanced Development, Security, and Operations (DevSecOps) practices, multi-disciplinary teams develop, operate, and maintain cloud-based applications using a robust suite of open-source and commercial tools. The project emphasizes automation, continuous integration and delivery, and strict adherence to federal security, privacy, and accessibility standards. Teams are responsible for end-to-end development, operations, security, and technical support, ensuring high-quality code, rapid incident response, and comprehensive documentation. This integrated approach enables USCIS to efficiently and securely manage complex identity and biometrics systems for both internal and public-facing applications.

opps.title: Visual Arts Support Services.
opps.description:
The U.S. Fire Administration (USFA), located at 16825 S. Seton Avenue, Emmitsburg, Maryland, requires visual arts support to provide professional graphic design services for USFA and NDEMU staff. The graphic and multimedia design staff will be located onsite at USFA.

Design staff will create visual assets that highlight and elevate USFAâ€™s brand, reinforcing its position as a trusted leader in fire and emergency medical services. Visual arts support services are expected to recommend best design practices.

The government will provide all necessary graphic design software and material supplies.

Design staff responsibilities include:

Publication layout and digital formatting
Training materials
Factsheets, handouts, flyers, and infographics
Illustrations
3D and 2D animations and simulations
Animated and motion graphics video productions
Program graphics and posters
Production work (large format printing, laminating, etc.)
Limited studio photography

opps.title: One Acquisition Solution for Integrated Services Plus (OASIS+) 8(a) Small Business Set-Aside Continuously Open Solicitation
opps.description:
May 12, 2026 The U.S. General Services Administration’s Office of Acquisition Solutions Development (ASD) is pleased to announce the first Phase II apparent awardee notifications for OASIS+ 8(a) Small Business Set-Aside. A consolidated list of Phase II apparent awardees for all OASIS+ contract families may be accessed on the Solicitations (continuously open) page (see “Links” section within this SAM.gov posting). This list will be updated with additional apparent awardees as award announcements continue to be made.

Award notifications have been sent via email to offerors who have been determined successful in one or more proposed domains. Apparent awardees are reminded to ensure that their SAM.gov entity information is active, accurate, and complete at all times. The program plans to issue formal awards and notices to proceed (NTPs) to qualified contractors on or about May 22, 2026 after final legal reviews occur. The newly awarded contracts will be available for ordering in GSA eBuy one business day following the issuance of formal awards and NTPs and upon contractor registration within the Vendor Support Center (VSC), whichever is later. Contract awardees must register their contracts in VSC before they will be visible in eBuy and available for procurement. 

Please note that GSA continues to release answers to RFP questions deemed broadly beneficial and relevant on a regular basis on the program website Solicitations (continuously open) page. Questions related to the award announcements should be submitted via email to the OASIS+ Contracting Officers at oasisplusmods@gsa.gov.

Please continue to monitor this OASIS+ Interact Community forum where you can join and subscribe to get notifications about announcements and program updates. For more information about the OASIS+ Program, visit: The OASIS+ website. Also, follow our social media channels X and LinkedIn. 

************************************************************************************

opps.title: NOS Cloud Migration & Data Infrastructure Support
opps.description: Comprehensive data management support to develop and implement a modern, interoperable ocean data infrastructure that will facilitate cloud migration, simplify data management, and accelerate AI integration across the organization. The core objective of this contract is to transition from traditional data architectures to a unifying multi-cloud environment, establishing governance standards and foundational support for AI development, including designing for the MLOps Lifecycle and simplifying AI access. This initiative is crucial for advancing NOS’s cloud-first strategy, supporting projects like marine critical minerals, and ensuring functional management of data and metadata. Potential vendors should structure their proposals to provide expertise in infrastructure engineering, data governance, and cloud architecture, covering positions such as Cloud Architect, Data Center Administrator, Data Technician, and IT Engineer.

opps.title: One Acquisition Solution for Integrated Services Plus (OASIS+) Total Small Business Set-Aside Continuously Open
opps.description:
May 12, 2026 The U.S. General Services Administration’s Office of Acquisition Solutions Development (ASD) is pleased to announce the first Phase II apparent awardee notifications for OASIS+ Total Small Business Set-Aside. A consolidated list of Phase II apparent awardees for all OASIS+ contract families may be accessed on the Solicitations (continuously open) page (see “Links” section within this SAM.gov posting). This list will be updated with additional apparent awardees as award announcements continue to be made.

Award notifications have been sent via email to offerors who have been determined successful in one or more proposed domains. Apparent awardees are reminded to ensure that their SAM.gov entity information is active, accurate, and complete at all times. The program plans to issue formal awards and notices to proceed (NTPs) to qualified contractors on or about May 22, 2026 after final legal reviews occur. The newly awarded contracts will be available for ordering in GSA eBuy one business day following the issuance of formal awards and NTPs and upon contractor registration within the Vendor Support Center (VSC), whichever is later. Contract awardees must register their contracts in VSC before they will be visible in eBuy and available for procurement. 

Please note that GSA continues to release answers to RFP questions deemed broadly beneficial and relevant on a regular basis on the program website Solicitations (continuously open) page. Questions related to the award announcements should be submitted via email to the OASIS+ Contracting Officers at oasisplusmods@gsa.gov.

Please continue to monitor this OASIS+ Interact Community forum where you can join and subscribe to get notifications about announcements and program updates. For more information about the OASIS+ Program, visit: The OASIS+ website. Also, follow our social media channels X and LinkedIn. 

***********************************************************************************

opps.title: Decennial Transformation & Application Modernization Acquisition
opps.description: The CenTAM Acquisition Framework supports the Business Ecosystem, the Decennial (2030 Census and early 2040 Census research), as well as IT, economic, geographic, demographic, research methodology programs, and other program areas within USCB. The purpose is to plan, develop, document, test, train, monitor, manage, and maintain software architecture and applications to support the 2030 Decennial Census operations. DTAM is the modernization of existing applications and the development of new applications, across Decennial systems, that will be leveraged for the Decennial Census Programs, in support of the 2028 Census Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Decennial Census.
win_reasons: Agency: BLN24 has past performance here (relationship + credibility advantage)
Partial cloud/modernization match — Clarity24 JV has NOAA cloud delivery experience
Small business set-aside

qualifying.q: Do we have the capabilities needed?

qualifying.answer: Forecast — early awareness, shape before RFP
$100.0M — Tier 2+ (BLN24 has won at this range)

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Census Bureau's massive digital modernization initiative — the CenTAM framework — covering IT systems for the 2030 Decennial Census. Includes application development, legacy modernization, cloud migration, data collection platforms, and systems to support economic, geographic, and demographic programs. Multiple separate contract awards expected under this framework.
rex_analysis.rex_take: Clarity24 JV has direct Census past performance ($4.5M MAF/TIGER modernization, $4.5M EDL Applications, $2.8M NOAA app mod). Census is also BLN24's highest-value client ($18.4M). This is a real opportunity — but it's a large technology modernization vehicle, not comms or HCD. The question is which specific task areas under CenTAM align with what Clarity24 can actually deliver vs. what larger primes will own. Full & Open means big integrators will compete.
rex_analysis.watch_notes: Get into the weeds on the CenTAM acquisition structure — it's a collection of complementary contracts, not one big award. Find which specific package maps to digital modernization vs. IT infrastructure vs. data engineering. Clarity24 should be positioned for the digital/app mod pieces. Due 2027 — shape now.

opps.description: Technical services contract for Model and Application Development support, including NBM, NWSConnect, Aviation model development, Storm Surge Model development, Warn on Forecast System (WoFS) development and Data dissemination services. Product development areas include: statistical modeling, digital database development; model interpretation, verification and evaluation; cloud infrastructure development and support; storm surge prediction; Virtual Lab infrastructure development and support, and decision support application development (NWS Connect). The goal of this modeling effort is to develop an accurate enough model guidance suite to allow it to reduce or eliminate the need for forecaster intervention, allowing the shift of focus of our limited forecast staff to support core partner decisions that have a greater impact on the mission to protect life and property.

opps.title: FY 26 USAFA Herbarium Digitization Project
opps.description:
SOURCES SOUGHT:

THIS IS NOT A REQUEST FOR QUOTATION; THIS NOTICE IS FOR INFORMATION AND PLANNING PURPOSES ONLY.

This notice does not constitute a commitment by the Government. All information submitted in response to this announcement is voluntary and the Government will not pay for information requested nor will it compensate any respondent for any cost incurred in developing the information provided to the Government.  

The Sources Sought number is FY26_USAFA_Herbarium_Digitization_Project and shall be used to reference any written responses to this sources sought.  

The United States Air Force Academy (USAFA) anticipates a requirement that is being considered under a small business set-aside program. The North American Industry Classification Systems (NAICS) Code proposed is 518210. The size standard for NAICS is $40M.

This requirement is for digitization service for USAFA Department of Biology Herbarium. Digitizing the herbarium specimens makes irreplaceable plant collections globally accessible, preserves fragile physical specimens by reducing handling, and enables large-scale data analysis for biodiversity research, conservation, and education, allowing scientists and the public to virtually access our plant specimens to answer a number of scientific questions. 

The government intends to solicit a firm-fixed price contract.

Delivery is FOB Destination to USAF Academy, CO 88040.



SALIENT CHARACTERISTICS/REQUIREMENT SPECIFICATIONS:  

At a minimum, the contractor performing the requirement must meet the following specifications:


	
	General & Scope:

	
		
		Provide a complete, end-to-end digitization solution for approximately 1,800-2,400 herbarium specimens housed in approximately 600 folders.
		
		
		Create high-quality, research-ready digital surrogates paired with structured, searchable metadata to enhance discovery and accessibility.
		
	
	
	
	Collection Handling & Preparation:

	
		
		Implement a barcoding system to digitally track the co

opps.title: Cyber Grant Support and Program Development
opps.description:
Summary of SOW Requirements: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS), via the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) Integrated Operations Division (IOD), requires the Contractor to deliver comprehensive support for the State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program (SLCGP) and Tribal Cybersecurity Grant Program (TCGP). The Contractor will actively advance program development, maintain information continuity, and ensure operational excellence across CISA regions, fostering strong coordination with CISA Divisions, Mission Enabling Offices, and interagency partners.

Scope and Objectives: The Contractor is responsible for both administrative and programmatic support to IOD, facilitating communication, capacity building, and awareness of cybersecurity grant programs. The Contractor will coordinate efforts across CISA regions and headquarters, strengthen regional capabilities, support service delivery, and execute IOD priorities.

Key Tasks and Requirements:
1. Program Support and Development: Collect and analyze best practices, lessons learned, and requirements to drive continuous improvement and inform future grant opportunities. Provide relevant documents and facilitate delivery of grant programs across regions, enhancing cyber competency among security advisors, analysts, and outreach staff. Deliver training to regional staff, ensuring readiness and effective execution. Establish mechanisms to capture return on investment (ROI) through program monitoring, and manage technical assistance for Cybersecurity Advisors and State Coordinators. Support annual program monitoring and grant close-out activities.

2. Operational and Strategic Engagement: Maintain and enhance tools such as the Service Change Request Tool and IOD Intake Process to gather requirements, prioritize services, and transition to full operating capability. Improve dashboards and automate reporting, including integration with ServiceNow. Develop engagement plans and public-facing material
win_reasons: Partial comms scope match — verify fit vs CMS/CDC/HUD delivery experience

⚠️ Gap area detected: Cybersecurity, Training & eLearning, CRM / Salesforce — would need a teaming partner for this scope

M67854-26-I-5111
Title: Database Services and Data Analysis Support for the United States Marine Corps (USMC) Corrosion Prevention and Control (CPAC) Program

Notice Type: Sources Sought / Request for Information (RFI)
Target GSA Schedule & SIN: Multiple Award Schedule (MAS) SIN 54151S (Information Technology Professional Services)
Anticipated NAICS Codes: 541512 (Computer Systems Design Services) or 541511 (Custom Computer Programming Services)

1.0 INTRODUCTION & BACKGROUND
This is a Request for Information (RFI) and Sources Sought notice issued solely for information and planning purposes; it does not constitute a Request for Proposal (RFP) or a promise to issue an RFP in the future. In accordance with 15.201(e), responses to this RFI are not offers and cannot be accepted by the Government to form a binding contract.  This notice is issued solely for market research and planning purposes, and the Government will not obligate any funds or reimburse respondents for any costs associated with preparing their submissions.

The United States Marine Corps (USMC) Corrosion Prevention and Control (CPAC) Program requires robust, secure, and highly analytical database services to support the tracking, management, and mitigation of corrosion across the enterprise. The CPAC program is critical to extending the lifecycle, ensuring the safety, and maintaining the combat readiness of USMC Ground Equipment.

The Government is seeking to identify qualified sources capable of providing specialized database management and analytical services, specifically those with demonstrated past performance working with USMC corrosion data and ground equipment platforms.

opps.title: CISA Stakeholder Engagement and Analytics Support Services
opps.description: The Stakeholder Engagement Division (SED) requires strategic relations, stakeholder engagement, analytics, and program support services to advance CISAâ€™s mission through coordinated outreach, partnership development, awareness initiatives, stakeholder data management, and performance evaluation. SED serves as CISAâ€™s focal point for enterprise stakeholder engagement, supporting relationships with federal, state, local, tribal, and territorial governments, critical infrastructure partners, private industry, academia, non-profit organizations, and the public. Support includes planning and executing stakeholder engagement strategies; managing awareness and outreach campaigns; supporting Systemically Important Entities (SIE) activities; facilitating Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure Act (CIRCIA) implementation efforts; supporting cybersecurity workforce and academic partnership initiatives; and maintaining stakeholder collaboration and information-sharing activities. The contractor shall provide expertise in stakeholder analysis, program evaluation, data governance, performance measurements, communications, and project management, including support for CISAâ€™s Stakeholder Relationship Management (SRM) platform, analytics, reporting and visualization capabilities. Support shall include collection, integration, analysis, and reporting of stakeholder and program data to assess outcomes, improve decision-making, identity trend, and enhance mission effectiveness. Work will be performed at a combination of contractor facilities and the Department of Homeland Security facilities as required.

opps.title: Management and Governance Directorate (MGD) Financial Analytics, Governance, and Acquisition Support
opps.description:
This re-compete acquisition seeks comprehensive professional support services for the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) Office of Information Technology (OIT)'s Management and Governance Directorate (MGD), Financial Management Acquisition Division (FMAD), and Strategic Planning & Mission Engagement Division (SPME). Services encompass strategy implementation, financial planning, and investment management, aiming to enhance OIT's budget formulation, planning, and reporting, and ensure transparent, data-driven financial decision-making. Key support areas include Capital Planning and Investment Control (CPIC) coordination, strategic financial planning, strategy management, and critical support for the Unified Immigration Portal (UIP) program, focusing on cost models and reporting. The requirement also involves extensive analytics, automation, data visualization, and platforms technical services, including the development and maintenance of executive dashboards (e.g., Power BI, Power Platform, Apptio Cloud ability). These services are essential for managing extensive financial resources and delivering mission-critical IT capabilities that support trade administration, traveler movement, and border security.

 Acquisition Strategy: 100% Small Business Set-Aside competed under General Services Administration (GSA) OASIS+ Small Business (SB) Pool.

opps.title: Program Management Office Support
opps.description: This requirement is for contractor support to provide three Agile-driven teams to deliver program management, risk management, and knowledge management services for the Student and Exchange Visitor Program (SEVP)systems and programs. The contractor will support performance measurement, process improvement, data analytics, requirements management, and business case development for SEVP operations. Responsibilities include developing and maintaining risk management plans for the Student and Exchange Visitor Information System (SEVIS) Programs Section and System Management Unit. The contractor support team will coordinate risk reviews, provide independent risk assessments, and manage risk inventories and mitigation strategies. Additionally, the contractor team will support the operational management of program documentation and knowledge libraries within the SEVP Assessment Management System (SEVPAMS), ensuring proper version control and metadata management. Strategic planning and administrative support will be provided to facilitate efficient operations, compliance with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) privacy and information assurance policies, and continuous improvement of SEVP systems and processes. The scope excludes software development and focuses on management, administration, and support functions.

opps.title: Updated: Enterprise Remote Document Authentication
opps.description:
*** 5/19/2026: USCIS anticipates releasing a solicitation on NASA SEWP V set-aside to small business and anticipates solicitation release in early June. This requirement will be competed without any brand-name specifications.***

USCIS anticipate a requirement for a commercial software solution for document authentication and identity verification service. These services are essential forÂ maintainingÂ program integrity, supporting federal operations, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements. Historically, many USCIS processes have relied on manual, in-person document review, which can present operational challenges and limit access and flexibility,Â especially for remote or distributed users. More specifically, the solution will capture high quality images of identity and work authorization documents, apply Presentation Attack Detection (PAD) and face match checks, use template and barcode/MRZ based authentication, and extract structured data via Optical Character Recognition (OCR) for use in USCIS-controlled microservices. All processing will occur within USCIS or approved Department of Homeland Security (DHS) environments, in compliance with DHS security and privacy requirements.

opps.title: USCG CGLO PROGRAM ANALYST
opps.description:
U.S. Coast Guard (USCG) USCG Liaison Office, situated onboard Naval Air Station Pensacola, FL at 211 South Avenue is seeking a Program/Management Analyst to fulfill its operational needs. This is a Firm-Fixed Price order.

The requirements document in the form of a Statement of Work is attached.

Detailed information regarding the incumbent award can be accessed on https://www.usaspending.gov/, under Award ID: 70Z04325FSMTC0005.

This announcement constitutes the only solicitation; quotations are being requested.
Evaluation: A Firm-Fixed purchase order will be made to the contractor that represents the best value. Quotes will be evaluated based on technical approach, experience of key personnel, and price. Under the 'Experience of Key Personnel' factor, the government will consider the experience of the proposed key person and the estimated time required to train them on government-specific systems and procedures. Proposals that demonstrate the key person has relevant experience and requires minimal training will be viewed more favorably.

The anticipated award date is 10 days after solicitation closing date.

All quoters must submit the following: Submission of a quote that does not contain all items requested below may result in elimination from consideration of award. Offerors must submit each item of quote in a separate electronic attachment clearly identified as described below. Offerors are responsible for including sufficient details to permit a complete and accurate evaluation of each quote.

DISCUSSIONS: The Government intends to award without discussions but reserves the right to conduct discussion should it be deemed in the Government's best interest.

VOLUME 1:
Section A Total price will be evaluated by the Government. Proposed price should reflect a clear understanding of the requirements, is consistent with the various elements of the offer.  

VOLUME 2 
Section A Technical Proposal: Offeror shall submit company's detailed technical approach and method

opps.title: Microsoft 365 Management, Operational Support, and Enhancements (MOSE)
opps.description:
USCIS has a requirement for Microsoft 365 Management, Operational Support, and Enhancements (MOSE). This Operations and Maintenance Services requirement will assist USCIS to operate, optimize, enhance and maintain existing M365 services, onboard new M365 services, and plan and develop strategies for future service sustainment, not limited to, but including: Core productivity applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, OneNote); Collaboration and Communication (Microsoft Team, SharePoint Online, OneDrive for Business, Loop, Whiteboard); Security and Compliance (Microsoft Purview, Defender, Entra ID, Intune, Rights Management and Information protection); Power Platform (Power Bi, Apps, Automate and Virtual Agents); Project and Task Management (Planner, To Do, List); Additional Services and Tools (Booking, Forms, Stream, Sway); Artificial Intelligence and Automation (Microsoft Copilot).  Assisting in planning and executing the rollout of Copilot /AI service in accordance with DHS and USCIS security and privacy policies as well as additional Microsoft 365 Services, USCIS may choose to adopt.   Additionally, USCIS needs support for M365 service integrations with both Microsoft and non-Microsoft Technologies, e.g., AirWatch, ServiceNow, Archive360, JAMF, and AgilQuest.

*Small business set aside to be determined through market research. USCIS intends to issue a Request for Information during May 2026.

opps.description:
This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial products prepared in accordance with RFO part 12. This announcement constitutes only solicitation. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation; offers are being requested and a separate written solicitation will not be issued.


	Solicitation number FA301626Q0139 is issued as a request for quotation (RFQ) for Purchase of Destruction Cart for the Joint Electromagnetic Warfare Center (JWEC) at Joint Base San Antonio (JBSA).
	The Governement shall solicit 100% Total Small business set aside under the assigned NAICS code.


Description of Product: The purpose of this statement of need is for a self-contained mobile, and secure data destruction system. The primary objective is to acquire a comprehensive solution capable of completely sanitizingand physically destroying both magnetic (rotational) and solid-state hard drives in accordance with National Security Agency (NSA) standards for handling Top Secret data. This capability is critical for ensuring compliance with data security protocols and mitigating the risk of data spillage. The system must provide a complete, verifiable audit trail of the erasure and destruction process for accountability and reporting purposes.

Question Submission Deadline: All questions must be submitted in writing by email no later than 11:00 AM Central Standard Time (CST) on TUESDAY, 23 June 2026. Response to questions will be posted by the Government on SAM.gov, contracting opportunities website as received. Questions submitted after this date/time will not be considered unless determined by the Contracting Officer.

Quote Submission Deadline: All quotes, along with the completed portions of the solicitation, must be received by 11:00 AM Central Standard Time (CST) on Friday, 26 June 2026. Quotes submitted after this date/time will not be considered unless determined by Contract Officer that accepting a quote after the submission deadline is the most advantageous for the govern

opps.title: Software Development, Operations, Maintenance (O&M) Support Services
opps.description:
The mission of the Health Resources and Services Administration (HRSA) is to improve health 
and achieve health equity through access to quality services, a skilled health workforce and 
innovative programs. HRSA assures the availability of quality health care to low income, 
uninsured, isolated, vulnerable, and special needs populations. As the world's leading public 
health agency, HRSA has the responsibility for promoting health and quality of life by 
preventing and controlling disease, injury and disability. HRSA works with States, local public 
health agencies and partners throughout the Nation and the world to accomplish this mission. 
HRSA serves as the national focus for developing and applying disease prevention and control 
methodologies, environmental health strategies, and health promotion and education activities 
designed to improve the health of the people of the United States and throughout the world. 
The contractor shall be capable of providing Software Development, Operations, Maintenance 
(O&M) support services for Custom, Enterprise Applications and Cloud Platform Systems as 
HRSA functions evolve or are incorporated into broader HHS enterprise or operating models, 
without interruption to services or degradation of security posture.

opps.title: Bioinformatics Analysis of Livestock Genomics
opps.agency: FDA Office of Acquisition and Grant Services
opps.description:
CVM possesses significant bioinformatics expertise; however, personnel with the specific skills to perform these tasks are currently allocated to other high-priority agency projects. To meet the urgent timelines for this analysis, CVM requires external, focused support to perform the necessary visual validation of swine variants from an already generated list of candidate variants and the generation of CRISPRme input files for cattle, chicken, and swine. This approach will allow the project to proceed efficiently while enabling CVM's internal experts to provide oversight and ensure the validated variants and CRISPRme input files meet their precise scientific requirements. 

Please see attached RFQ.
opps.summary:

 The Center for Veterinary Medicine (CVM) at the FDA is seeking external support for bioinformatics analysis due to personnel being allocated to other high-priority projects. 

 The goal is to validate genomic variants in CRISPR/Cas9 gene-edited swine samples and generate CRISPRme input files for cattle, chicken, and swine. This will allow CVM's internal experts to oversee the project while ensuring that the scientific requirements are met efficiently.

Work Details
 The contractor will perform the following tasks: 
1) Visual validation of genomic variants in whole genome sequencing

opps.title: National Incident Management System (NIMS) Implementation, Resource Typing, National Qualification System (NQS) Development, and Administrative Support.
opps.description: The work to be completed under this contract advances the implementation of the National Preparedness System (NPS) and NIMS, to include: 1) developing materials to support NIMS implementation, 2) developing and producing new or updated NIMS resource typing documents, 3) supporting the development of the NQS, and 4) developing and implementing a strategy to collect and analyze preparedness data across National Integration Center (NIC) program areas. These four focus areas are central to the nation's ability to build, sustain, and deliver the core capabilities in support of the National Preparedness Goal and System. The NIMS resource typing and NQS documents are essential to ensuring the maximum level of mission readiness at the local, state, tribal, territorial, and federal levels for day-to-day mutual aid and preparedness for major disasters.  The contractor shall provide the NIC with technical, administrative, and programmatic support services, as specified in the Performance Work Statement (PWS).

- Recommend, develop, and assist in implementing strategies and action plans that optimize adoption and effective organizational change management (OCM).

Business Performance
- Assist in developing, implementing, and routinely updating performance measurement systems, metrics frameworks, and toolsets that deliver capabilities that monitor, control, visualize, and maintain organizational functionality.
- Perform data Extract-Transform-Load (ETL) procedures

opps.description: SETSS IDIQ 2.0 (Recompete)
win_reasons: Agency: BLN24 has past performance here (relationship + credibility advantage)
Small business set-aside
No direct capability lane match found — scope may not align with BLN24 proven delivery
opps.vehicle_note: Contract VEHICLE (IDIQ/GWAC/on-ramp): award is by self-scoring qualification thresholds, not head-to-head competition. Win-prob %, competitor lists, and listed value are not meaningful here — the real question is whether BLN24 clears the domain scorecard.

capability_matrix.fit_reason: No task areas identified in scope text — review solicitation directly

qualifying.answer: Forecast — early awareness, shape before RFP
$35.0M — Tier 2+ (BLN24 has won at this range)

rex_analysis.what_they_need: NOAA's vehicle for Science, Engineering, and Technical Support Services across its research offices — weather, oceans, fisheries, atmospheric research. Task orders span scientific program support, systems engineering, data management, IT systems, and environmental compliance across ~235 NOAA facilities.
rex_analysis.rex_take: Clarity24 JV has $7M in confirmed NOAA delivery (cloud migration, app modernization), which is real past performance. But SETSS is primarily a science and engineering vehicle — meteorology, oceanography, fisheries, facilities. BLN24's lanes (HCD, comms, data analytics) are narrow within this IDIQ. Best path is monitoring for specific task orders in digital/data/comms scope rather than pursuing the IDIQ prime. Due Jan 2027 — early awareness only.
rex_analysis.watch_notes: Watch for task order RFPs under the existing SETSS vehicle that align with digital, UX, or data analytics scope. Clarity24's NOAA relationship is the asset here. Avoid chasing the IDIQ prime unless a teaming partner with deep NOAA scientific credentials is in place.

opps.description:
This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial products and commercial services as prepared in accordance with the format in Revolutionary Federal Acquisition Regulation Overhaul (RFO) Subpart 12.212(b)(1), as supplemented in the attached Request for Quotation (RFQ). This announcement constitutes the only solicitation. A firm-fixed price purchase order is anticipated.  RFO 12, Acquisition of Commercial Products and Commercial Services will be used. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation. 
The solicitation number is 3626027Q0003 and is attached.
This solicitation is set aside for small businesses. This solicitation incorporates provisions and clauses by reference. The full text of provisions and clauses may be accessed electronically at www.acquisition.gov. 
The associated North American Industrial Classification System (NAICS) code for this procurement is 541519, with a small business size standard of $34 Million.
VA Portland Healthcare System, 3710 SW U.S. Veterans Hospital Road, Portland, OR 97239-2964 is seeking physician scheduling software brand name or equal to Amion in accordance with Section B.2, Price/Cost Schedule and Section, D.1, Statement of Work.
The period of performance will be a base plus four option years
Base:    10/01/2026 - 09/30/2027
Option Year 1:  10/01/2027 - 09/30/2028
Option Year 2:  10/01/2028 - 09/30/2029

Award will be based upon the evaluation factors listed in Section E of the attached RFQ. The award will be made to the response most advantageous to the Government. Responses should contain your best terms and conditions. 
Offers must be emailed to Jennifer Robles jennifer.robles1@va.gov and received no later than 1:00 PM Mountain on 07/16/2026. 
Questions to this solicitation shall be submitted via electronic mail to the Contracting Officer Jennifer Robles (jennifer.robles1@va.gov). Questions shall be accepted until 1:00 PM Mountain on 07/06/2026. The Government is not obligated to respond to any questions receiv

opps.title: CONFIGURATION MANAGEMENT (CM) & INFRASTRUCTURE SUPPORT SERVICES 19AQMM26Q0278
opps.description:
Configuration Management and Infrastructure Support Services

The U.S. Department of State, Bureau of Diplomatic Technology, Enterprise Applications, Personnel and Training Applications (DT/EA/PTA), is seeking Configuration Management (CM) and Infrastructure Support Services in support of the Integrated Personnel Management System (IPMS). This acquisition is a competitive 8(a) set-aside under the GSA Multiple Award Schedule (MAS) program, issued pursuant to FAR 8.405-3 ordering procedures.

The Government intends to award a single Labor-Hour task order to support the following functional areas:

Configuration management, source code version control, and package build support for multiple development teams
Application development, modification, maintenance, and testing of information systems, subsystems, and databases
Infrastructure architecture support, including systems design, administration, maintenance, and support of physical and virtual servers in a 24/7 enterprise production environment. Senior development support for all aspects of PeopleSoft technical activities, including software architecture, technical design, programming, unit testing, and system testing

Period of Performance: Base Period: September 1, 2026 August 31, 2027, with one 12-month option period (September 1, 2027 August 31, 2028).

Place of Performance: Washington, DC

Contract Type: Labor-Hour

NAICS Code: 541511 Custom Computer Programming Services (Size Standard: $34 million)

Security Requirement: Quoters must possess an active Secret Facility Clearance (FCL) at the time of quotation submission. All contractor personnel performing under the task order shall possess, at minimum, an active Secret personnel security clearance prior to beginning performance.

Eligibility: This acquisition is restricted to SBA 8(a) certified firms holding an active GSA Multiple Award Schedule (MAS) contract encompassing the required labor categories and services. Quoters must be active and eligible 8(a) parti

The Contractor shall furnish the necessary personnel and services to perform the Performance-Based Work Statement (PWS) tasks.  This TO covers Small and Large Database Migration Support, Benchmarking, Optimization, and Performance Tuning as well as Migration Metrics, Code Conversion, Database Queries, and Report Generation, while leveraging existing technology that improves services and provides better customer service experiences. opps.title: Spectrum Valuation
opps.description: NTIA seeks contractor support for spectrum valuation to design and deliver a defensible, repeatable, and data driven valuation of designated radio-frequency spectrum bands to inform strategic planning, budgeting, policy development, and potential reallocation/sharing decisions. The effort will produce an analytical framework, an economic model, market analysis, operational utility assessment, and valuation outputs with clear decision support for leadership. A standardized valuation methodology—grounded in economic, technical, regulatory, and operational factors—is needed to support investment, sharing, auction participation, relocation cost analysis, and long term spectrum strategy.

Evaluation assesses program design, implementation, and outcomes using rigorous research methodology.
rex_analysis.rex_take: BLN24 is stronger here than the initial score suggests. IRS SB/SE BOD Leadership Initiative ($2.7M via Clarity24) is a DIRECT workforce development / human capital reference — leadership development, culture assessment for 60+ IRS executives, organizational transformation, capacity building. That directly maps to evaluating workforce training programs. EDARMST $2.5M is same agency (IES). Fors Marsh JV covers evaluation methodology via DHRA $59M behavioral research. Teaming play: BLN24/Clarity24 leads workforce dev + data analytics, Fors Marsh JV leads evaluation design. MRAS response is low-effort. Respond now.

opps.description:
On behalf of Department of Homeland Security (DHS), GSA is conducting market research.

DO NOT submit your response in eBuy. Your response will not be included in the report that is provided to the customer. NOTE: If the link does not work, try clearing your browser history (cache) and then copying and pasting the link into a new browser. To respond to this RFI, you MUST copy and paste the below link into a new browser.

Only responses submitted through the linked RFI will be accepted. Nothing else will be evaluated or recorded (this includes selecting 'interested' within eBuy).


Link to RFI: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_1TFATBJBgcWGe8K


Additional Information:
Due to the structure of contracts in GSA eBuy, you may receive multiple Sources Sought or RFI notifications for the same requirement if it falls under multiple of your company's awarded SINs. However, please note that you are only required to respond to the RFI once.

Be sure to use the URL provided above to submit your response.


Any questions regarding this RFI will be responded to by email only.  Send your inquiry to: rfi@research.gsa.gov and be sure to include the RFQ number.

 The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is conducting market research on behalf of the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) to support its Open Architecture (OA) initiative. opps.description:
**Amendment 0001 is being issued to provide answers to the questions submitted (Attachment 7) and incorporate revisions (Attachments 1 - 6).  As a result, the proposal due date has been extended to 8 July 2026 at 2:00 PM EDT.  Offerors shall provide a signed copy of this amendment as part of the proposal acknowledging all changes.**



USSOCOM has a requirement for Program Management Office Support Services (PMOSS) in support of USSOCOM J6.  This requirement provides management, planning, and financial support that helps USSOCOM oversee programs, resources, budgets, contracts, and organizational initiatives. The contractor assists government leadership with planning future requirements, tracking performance, managing documentation, monitoring contractor services, coordinating working groups and meetings, analyzing data, supporting budget execution, and helping ensure programs are carried out efficiently and in accordance with government policies and objectives. The contract serves as a support function that enables informed decision-making, effective resource management, and successful execution of USSOCOM priorities.
opps.summary:

opps.title: 2030 Decennial Administrative Recruiting, Hiring, and Training System (DARHTS)
opps.description:
The Census Integrated Personnel and Payroll System (CIPPS) is an integrated suite of government and vendor systems for recruiting, assessing, fingerprinting, background clearance, hiring, onboarding, benefits, and training temporary employees* in support of the 2028 Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Decennial Census. 

The purpose of this requirement is to obtain technical contractor services for the 2030 Decennial Administrative, Recruiting, Hiring, and Training System (DARHTS). The 2030 DARHTS is the primary Census system for automating, standardizing, and streamlining of job applications and assessments during the 2028 Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Census. This requirement will also obtain a robust, integrated training solution and Help Desk (optional) operations during the 2028 Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Census. The provided training solution shall utilize a FedRAMP (Moderate or Higher) compliant Software as a Service (SaaS) solution.

Note that the Solicitation along with Attachments 1 through 3 and 5 through 14 are attached. Attachment 4 is not included with this solicitation and will be provided after award.

Note that this is a Hybrid Type Contract with Firm Fixed Price (FFP) components as well as Time and Material (T&M) components.

opps.title: Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Case Management System (CMS)
opps.description: The DHA Office of Equal Employment and Resolution Management (OEERM) office requires this automated tracking system to ensure accurate management and oversight of EEO Complaints for the Agency or the production of the annual 462 report in accordance with regulatory compliance. The goal of this requirement is to continue to maintain this commercial-off-the-shelf (COTS) product (Case Management Development Platform powered by Entellitrak) in order to provide a system capable of ensuring the agency is within regulatory compliance. Similar products will be implemented for other capabilities such as Anti-Harassment (AH). This COTS solution will continue to be hosted on Military Cloud (milCloud) development, test and production environments. Additional responsibility for this effort will be to provide operations, sustainment and engineering support as well as system updates and maintenance of all non-production environments.

The requirement encompasses the delivery of brand-name or equal equipment that meets or exceeds all technical specifications identified in the Statement of Work (SOW).
CISA requires modern, secure, and reliable IT hardware solutions that directly support all Agency personnel in executing their mission. The scope of this requirement includes, but is not limited to:

- Laptops and desktop computers

- Keyboards and mice
- External PIV card readers

All equipment must comply with applicable CISA security, performance, and compatibility standards and must integrate seamlessly with the Agencyâ€™s existing IT environment.

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Department of Education Institute of Education Sciences (IES) needs a contractor to evaluate the effectiveness of Workforce Pell Grants — a federal program allowing Pell Grant funding for short-term workforce-focused training. Evaluation assesses program design, implementation, and outcomes using rigorous research methodology.
rex_analysis.rex_take: BLN24 is stronger here than the initial score suggests. IRS SB/SE BOD Leadership Initiative ($2.7M via Clarity24) is a DIRECT workforce development / human capital reference — leadership development, culture assessment for 60+ IRS executives, organizational transformation, capacity building. That directly maps to evaluating workforce training programs. EDARMST $2.5M is same agency (IES). Fors Marsh JV covers evaluation methodology via DHRA $59M behavioral research. Teaming play: BLN24/Clarity24 leads workforce dev + data analytics, Fors Marsh JV leads evaluation design. MRAS response is low-effort. Respond now.
rex_analysis.watch_notes:
Respond via Qualtrics form: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_8bPc93UqNaEsfIi
Highlight: IRS SB/SE Leadership Initiative (Clarity24 $2.7M) for workforce dev, EDARMST $2.5M for same-agency IES past performance, Fors Marsh JV for evaluation methodology.

opps.title: FISMA Assessment Services
opps.description: The requirement is for a contractor to conduct a full security and privacy control assessment, covering 100% of the controls for a Moderate / Moderate / Moderate FISMA system that has a High Value Asset overlay. The successful vendor must demonstrate the necessary skills and in-depth knowledge to ensure the assessment fully complies with federal mandates, including DOC and NOAA policies, and NIST requirements (such as NIST SP 800-37 Rev 2 and NIST SP 800-53A Rev 5). The primary goal is to produce accurate assessment findings that support an Authority to Operate (ATO) recommendation.1
win_reasons: Agency: BLN24 has past performance here (relationship + credibility advantage)
No direct capability lane match found — scope may not align with BLN24 proven delivery
⚠️ Scope gap: Cybersecurity — BLN24 has no confirmed prime delivery in these lanes

pp_refs: IRS Human Centered Design & Research BPA ($7M)
Dept of Education EDARMST Data Analytics ($2.9M)

opps.title: Information Assurance Security Services III  (IASS3)
opps.description:
***2/9/2026: CO updated from Andrew Fonda to Celina Hemingway.***
***1/15/2026: Strategic Sourcing Vehicle updated to 8(a) STARS III; Estimated period of performance end updated from 7/30/29 to 6/13/31; Estimated solicitation release date updated from 12/30/26 to 8/21/26; Alternate contact updated from Sylwia Salkic to Andrew Fonda.

***7/23/2025 Update: POC updated from Christopher Green to Megan Kane.

USCIS has a requirement for Information Assurance Security Services (IASS). As USCIS integrates artificial intelligence (AI) / machine learning (ML) and other emerging technologies into our operations, it is imperative to elevate our cyber vigilance. This means ensuring systems are not only resilient to adversarial attacks but also uphold ethical standards that reflect organizational values and societal expectations. The Information Security Division (ISD) seeks a next generation of ISSOs to lead the charge. The IASS resources will continue to perform the day-to-day implementation and validation of IT Security requirements, including ensuring the protection of our systems, data, and environment from malicious users, and to support continuous monitoring activities. These specialized ISSOs are critical to maintaining the security of our systems, assets, and data to avoid or minimize the impact of events.
win_reasons: Strong data/analytics scope match — BLN24 proven prime (Census SRQA AI/ML, Education EDARMST risk analytics)
8(a) set-aside — direct vehicle match for BLN24
OUT OF SCOPE: vehicle — not in BLN24 delivery lanes
⚠️ Scope gap: AI / Machine Learning, Cybersecurity — BLN24 has no confirmed prime delivery in these lanes
matched_lanes: Data/Analytics (Census $4.1M SRQA, Dept of Education $2.9M — proven prime)
opps.vehicle_note: Contract VEHICLE (IDIQ/GWAC/on-ramp): award is by self-scoring qualification thresholds, not head-to-head competition. Win-prob %, competitor lists, and listed value are not meaningful here — the real question is whether BLN24 clears the domain scorecard.

tasks.note: BLN24 gap — no confirmed prime cybersecurity delivery; would need dedicated cyber partner

capability_matrix.fit_reason: All identified task areas fall outside BLN24's proven delivery lanes

qualifying.q: Do we have the capabilities needed?

qualifying.answer: Forecast — early awareness, shape before RFP
$35.0M — Tier 2+ (BLN24 has won at this range)

opps.title: Security Operation Analytics Rigor (SOAR)
opps.description:
***5/28/26 - updated set-aside, Solicitation Release Date, and Period of Performanace***

The U.S. Citizenship & Immigration Services (USCIS) has a requirement for Security Operation Analytics Rigor (SOAR), consisting of teams to provide development, security, operations (DevSecOps), data processing and analytic services to support USCIS, Information Technology (IT) development delivery services. These services primarily provide identification of Fraud, National Security and Public Safety concerns. The teams focus will be on performing development, security, and operational support activities. The team will also perform, enhancements and refinements of algorithms as well as standardizing large-scale, complex, internal and external applications. 

All applications in the portfolio employ a baseline set of DevSecOps development postures along with USCIS required development gates, safeguards and requirements. They can be categorized into several overarching categories: Case Management, Investigation, Data Storage / Operations, Screening and Vetting, Immigration Operations, Immigration Law Enforcement, and Intelligence Operations.

*Currently performing market research*
win_reasons: Partial cloud/modernization match — Clarity24 JV has NOAA cloud delivery experience
Small business set-aside
OUT OF SCOPE: ship — not in BLN24 delivery lanes
⚠️ Scope gap: DevSecOps — BLN24 has no confirmed prime delivery in these lanes

tasks.note: BLN24 prime — Census SRQA, MAF/TIGER, EDL all heavy data engineering
Adjacent (2/4) — Census SRQA used agile + DevSecOps principles; IRS Forms Mod used CI/CD pipelines. Not standalone DevSecOps prime but confirmed delivery within broader contracts.
BLN24 gap — no confirmed prime cybersecurity delivery; would need dedicated cyber partner

capability_matrix.fit_reason: Majority of task areas require teaming — may not be best lead

qualifying.q: Do we have the capabilities needed?

qualifying.answer: Forecast — early awareness, shape before RFP
$50050.0M — Tier 2+ (BLN24 has won at this range)

opps.description: NOS requires comprehensive support to ensure organizational compliance with Department of Commerce (DOC), NOAA, and FISMA policies by providing technical and non-technical cybersecurity assistance and consultation. Vendors are expected to provide qualified labor to maintain enterprise-level dashboards, validate user permissions for operational tools such as Tenable Security Center, and provide expert guidance based on NIST Special Publications to improve IT operations and reduce risk. opps.description:
On behalf of Department of Commerce (DOC), GSA is conducting market research.

DO NOT submit your response in eBuy. Your response will not be included in the report that is provided to the customer. NOTE: If the link does not work, try clearing your browser history (cache) and then copying and pasting the link into a new browser. To respond to this RFI, you MUST copy and paste the below link into a new browser.

Only responses submitted through the linked RFI will be accepted. Nothing else will be evaluated or recorded (this includes selecting 'interested' within eBuy).


Link to RFI: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_3vHEA7ULWARVrOm


Additional Information:
Due to the structure of contracts in GSA eBuy, you may receive multiple Sources Sought or RFI notifications for the same requirement if it falls under multiple of your company's awarded SINs. However, please note that you are only required to respond to the RFI once.

Be sure to use the URL provided above to submit your response.


Any questions regarding this RFI will be responded to by email only.  Send your inquiry to: rfi@research.gsa.gov and be sure to include the RFQ number.
opps.summary:
Background
 The U.S. Department of Commerce (DOC) through the National Telecommunications and Information Administration (NTIA) is initiating the Public Wireless Supply Chain Innovation Fund (PWSCIF), a $1.5 billion discretionary grant program aimed at accelerating the commercial development of open-interface, standards-based, compatible, interoperable equipment.

This initiative focuses on enhancing the development of AI RAN, Open Radio Access Network (Open RAN) hardware, software, and micro-processing technology to facilitate secure and interoperable fifth-generation (5G) wireless networks.
win_reasons: Agency: BLN24 has past performance here (relationship + credibility advantage)
8(a) set-aside — direct vehicle match for BLN24

opps.description:
CISAâ€™s Stakeholder Engagement Division requires a CRITICAL INFRASTRUCTURE ENGAGEMENT AND ADVISORY SUPPORT SERVICES contract for support to plan, coordinate, manage, and sustain a range of activities associated with critical infrastructure engagement, national-level coordination, and advisory bodies.  Within CISA, the Stakeholder Engagement Division (SED) executes critical responsibilities related to planning, coordinating, and sustaining the Agencyâ€™s engagement with councils, advisory bodies, committees, and other formal governance structures that support national-level critical infrastructure security and resilience.  To execute these functions effectively, SED requires contractor support to plan, manage, operationalize, document, and sustain the full lifecycle of council and advisory activities, as well as to facilitate cross-council coordination, strategic communication, and records and governance management essential to CISAâ€™s broader responsibilities. The forthcoming contract will provide support across:
Â·        Council and Advisory Body Management: Lifecycle management for councils, committees, working groups, and governance artifacts.
Â·        National-Level Coordination Construct Support: Support to evolving cross-sector and interagency governance mechanisms.
Â·        Strategic and Operational Engagement Support: Planning, coordination, logistics, documentation, and execution of classified and unclassified engagements.
Â·        Research, Analysis, and Policy Support: Development of reports, white papers, analyses, dashboards, and executive communications supporting national critical infrastructure priorities.

opps.title: Enterprise Gateway and Integration Services (EGIS) III
opps.description:
USCIS has a requirement for Enterprise Gateway and Integration Services (EGIS) III. Historically, EGIS has served as the enterprise integration backbone connecting USCIS internal systems with Department of Homeland Security (DHS), interagency, and external partners. While this core mission remains critical, EGIS will also expand this role supporting national security, immigration system integrity and advanced adjudication operations with key mission including: National security vetting and evolving immigration policies; screening immigration benefit applicants; protecting immigration benefits and work authorization integrity; advanced data integration, analytics, AI-enabled anomaly detection, and decision support; agility, rapid delivery, operational excellence, transparency, and reduced duplication; fraud detection, risk scoring, and investigative data workflows. 
As of 3/3/2026 we are in the market research/ acquisition planning phase of this acquisition, as such, some of the APFS fields may change over the coming months. This record will continually be updated once changes to the strategy are known.

opps.title: USACE UMSB Army Vantage Integration Field Service Representative (FRS)
opps.description: The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, USACE Mission Support Battalion (UMSB) is executing a directed modernization initiative to transition operational workflows from manual, locally managed processes to structured, enterprise-integrated systems aligned with Army Vantage and the Microsoft ecosystem. This effort supports federal and Army digital transformation mandates requiring measurable progress in data consolidation, automated reporting, and enterprise visibility. One (1) senior-level Field Service Representative (FSR) will need to be embedded at Humphreys Engineer Center, Alexandria, Virginia, with periodic presence at a secondary Government location in Washington, DC beginning in Month 4. The FSR shall perform duties consistent with the approved Performance Work Statement (PWS) across five task areas: backend integration support, workflow documentation and transition support, user training and adoption support, integration status reporting, and stakeholder engagement and coordination. The FSR shall provide on-site technical and change management support, working directly with workflow owners, UMSB leadership, and the UMSB Operations (OPS) office. The overarching program objective for the base period is to achieve the HR Integration End State as defined in Section 3 of the Performance Work Statement (PWS), establishing a replicable integration model for future directorate-level transitions. Leadership's primary operational objective is to utilize Army Vantage's built-in templated dashboard functions, populated from authoritative HR data sources, as the standard method for HR data visibility and decision support, replacing manual data pulls and locally managed reporting.

opps.description:
6/22/2026 - Amendment 0003 provides additional clarification to the proposal preparation General Instructions. Please refer to the SF30 for additional details.

6/17/2026 - Amendment 0002 revises Section L and Section M of the RFP. Please see the SF30 continuation page for the list of changes.

6/9/2026 - Amendment 0001 attachments and Responses to RFP Questions and Comments are posted. Proposal due date extended to June 26, 2026.

1. Introduction and Background

The mission of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration’s (NOAA) National Environmental Satellite, Data, and Information Service (NESDIS) is to provide and ensure timely access to global environmental data from satellites and other sources to promote, protect, and enhance the Nation's economy, security, environment, and quality of life. NESDIS acquires and manages the Nation's operational environmental satellites, provides data and information services to numerous end users, and conducts related research.

Since 1990, NCEI’s RCS program has traditionally supported Regional Weather Records Centers (RWRCs) established at Public Universities within six geographic regions. For more than forty years, Regional Centers have produced and delivered environmental data, products, information, and services – hereafter collectively termed “products and services” – and captured user needs. By transforming, tailoring, and augmenting NCEI’s products and services, the RWRCs provide a bridge between NCEI and regional user communities, including the private sector. The RWRCs support NOAA’s mission to advance understanding of long-term weather variability and impacts through user engagement and the translation of data into usable products and services for decision makers.

2. Scope of Work

A. Geographic Scope

This contract is for the Southeast Regional Weather Records Center (SERWRC), which is composed of: VA, NC, SC, GA, AL, FL, Puerto Rico (PR), and US Virg

opps.title: R408--Radiation Oncology Assessment Services
opps.description:
STATEMENT OF WORK




	GENERAL INFORMATION



	Title of Project: Radiation Oncology Services Assessment Services for National Radiation Oncology Program, (NROP) 11SPEC22.


2. Scope of Work:  The essential services to be performed by a subject matter expert with knowledge and experience with VHA National Radiation Oncology Program. The contractor will perform the following tasks:

2.1. Review and Recommendations:

 Comparison of non-VA radiation oncology facility practices with NROP Office established performance guidelines and providing recommendations for approvals, and coordination of approval notifications to community practices.

2.2. Coordination of Chart Reviews:

The contractor shall provide specialized coordination and administrative clinical support to facilitate physician Ongoing Professional Practice Evaluations (OPPE) and focused chart review activities for radiation oncology sites that operate with limited physician staffing, including single-physician facilities.  The services are essential to ensure continuity of care, regulatory compliance, and maintenance of high-reliability clinical operations in highly complex radiotherapy environments.   

2.3. Data Management:

 Perform complex, short-term projects for NROP to evaluate credentialing data reports and to provide some development assistance for NROP to manage data for purposes of reporting at micro and macro levels.

2.4. Conference Coordination:

Organize and manage logistics for conferences hosted by the National Radiation Oncology Program, including conference approvals, contacting speakers, and handling pre- and post-conference relevant tasks.   

2.5 Conduct Surveys:

Conduct customer satisfaction surveys in the field as required by SCPO and the NROP.


	Background: The National Radiation Oncology Program provides clinical oversite for the 41 VHA radiation oncology services/sections, setting policies for radiation therapy delivery and organizing and developing current infrastructure.


Since
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Radiation Oncology Program (NROP) provides clinical oversight for the 41 VHA radiation oncology services/sections, setting policies for radiation therapy delivery and developing current infrastructure. 

Since 2020, there has been a significant increase in NROP initiatives, including quality surveillance and legislative proposals. The administrative integrity of the program requires assessment services to ensure best practices for ongoing needs.

The DHS Office of Privacy requires contractor support to analyze, standardize, and transform over 4,500+ legacy privacy compliance artifacts (including Privacy Threshold Analysis, Privacy Impact Assessment, and related documentation) into a modernized Privacy Risk Framework. The office is implementing a new standardized template framework aligned with the Privacy Act, E-Government Act of 2002, OMB guidance, and the NIST Privacy Framework. This effort is not limited to document conversion.
The contractor shall:
â€¢	Transform legacy compliance artifacts into structured, analyzable data
â€¢	Map content to the Privacy Risk Framework domains and scoring criteria
â€¢	Identify privacy risks, mitigations, and control gaps
â€¢	Enable future system ingestion and automation
â€¢	Support development of repeatable, scalable workflows
â€¢	Provide decision-support outputs for leadership

opps.title: Google Workspace Enterprise Plus
opps.description:
This procurement, supporting the Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO), is for the use of Google Workspace Enterprise Plus Apps to support CISAâ€™s IT Operations performance functions. The Enterprise Plus apps are a cloud-based browser with built-in controls that provide encryption and verification which enables work from anywhere and eliminates the need for VPNs. The apps have the ability to operate on a global scale while protecting information from phishing, malware, ransomware, and supply chain attacks. And the apps have secure endpoints that do not require patching and strong account takeover protections.

The software will be used to analyze CISA datasets to enhance leadership awareness of trends and organizational performance. The software procured must be capable of conducting real- time analysis; be relatively easy to use; allow data to flow easily into and out of the software; have forecasting capabilities that can predict values based on trends in the dataset; utilize user- friendly dashboards to format visualizations; and support professional reporting formats and data visualizations suitable for executive-level presentations.

 The contractor shall provide the CISA Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO) with the following: 
1. Google Workspace Enterprise Plus; New â€“ Quantity: 1,300, 
2. Google Workspace Enterprise Plus; Archived â€“ Quantity: 1,300, 
3. G Suite Support Base Fee; - Quantity: 1, 
4. G Suite Support Variable Fee; - Quantity: 1,
5. Cloud Platform â€“ Support G Suite; Quantity: 50,000

opps.title: OER - NOAA Ship Telepresence (FY27 - FY30)
opps.description: This license will provide video/imagery telepresence support for OER Ocean Expeditions on NOAA Ship Okeanos Explorer, and replacement NOAA Ship Discoverer and other platforms/projects; satellite upload / download ship to shore of large data volumes and video streams, with critical live communication to/from shore subject matter experts, universities, cooperative institutes, and outside agencies to expand exploration and understanding of the ocean environment, and allow for immediate awareness and distribution of findings to the ocean community at-large. While video is archived at NOAA NCEI, there is no comparable viewable portal to access the video for the ocean community at large or to reach global subject matter experts to identify findings. This mechanism serves as the publicly viewable access point for video and images that are acquired on the NOAA Ship and through other OER funded projects. Annotated video data from other platforms

opps.title: Supporting NIHHIS:Advancing Heat Resilience through Data, Policy,and Partnerships
opps.description: Assessing heat-related impacts in rural and coastal areas and in six sectors: energy, health, transportation, agriculture, housing, and labor. This award advances local and state heat preparedness by expanding to two new sectors: tourism and small business, as well as hosting new private sector engagements and delivering new products that state and local governments can use to improve heat preparedness. This work is critical to delivering on NOAA/National Integrated Heat Health Information System (NIHHIS)’s mission to improve decision-making and reduce heat-related deaths and other impacts to the economy.

opps.title: DTMO Travel Operations Management Support Services (TOMSS)
opps.agency: Office of the Secretary of Defense
opps.description:
The Defense Travel Management Office (DTMO) requires enterprise wide operational
management and support for the Integrated Lodging Programs that will enable
organizational leadership to make informed, fact-based decisions that positively impact the daily
execution of mission requirements and the development of longer-term strategies. The Travel
Programs Branch's strategic objective is to adopt lodging industry best practices to
leverage strategic sourcing opportunities; and to fully employ the strategic sourcing management
philosophy to better meet DoD's requirements while generating savings for the Department of
Defense.  This will be a Firm-Fixed-Price contract with Time-and-Material CLINS for Travel.
opps.summary:
Background
 The Defense Travel Management Office (DTMO), part of the Defense Human Resources Activity (DHRA), is seeking enterprise-wide operational management and support for the Integrated Lodging Programs. Established to centralize commercial travel within the Department of War (DoW), DTMO aims to leverage strategic sourcing opportunities and adopt industry best practices to enhance lodging services for DoW travelers. 

This contract is a follow-on requirement to an existing contract, emphasizing the need for informed decision-making in daily mission execution and long-term strategy develop

opps.title: Maintenance of ISO 9001: Certification Audits
opps.description:
This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial services prepared in accordance with the format in FAR Subpart 12.6, as supplemented with additional information included in this notice. This announcement constitutes only the solicitation; proposals are being requested, and a written solicitation will NOT be issued. 

This is a Request for Quote (RFQ) using FAR Part 12, Acquisition of Commercial Services; the Solicitation Number is W519TC-26-Q-ISO2.

The solicitation and incorporated provisions and clauses are those in effect through the FAR. 

The NAICS code for this procurement is 541611; the small business standard is $25,000,000.00. The Product Service Code is R704.

Offerors must be registered in System for Award Management (SAM) database at time of response to this solicitation. The website for SAM is https://www.sam.gov/
Addendum to 52.212-1.

DESCRIPTION OF THE REQUIREMENT

This solicitation is issued for Maintenance of ISO 9001: Certification Audits at Crane Army Ammunition Activity (CAAA), in accordance with the Performance Work Statement as Attachment 0001. 

The Contract Line Item Number (CLIN) Structure is as follows:
CLIN 0001 Stage Audit 1 (S1)
CLIN 0002 Stage Audit 2 (S2)
CLIN 0003 Travel Year 1 Audits
CLIN 0004 Surveillance Audit 3 (SA3)
CLIN 0005 Surveillance Audit 4 (SA4)
CLIN 0006 Travel Year 2 Audits
CLIN  0007 Surveillance Audit 5 (SA5)
CLIN 0008 Recertification Audit (RC1)
CLIN 0009 Travel Year 3 Audits

TYPE OF CONTRACT, PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE and PERFORMANCE LOCATION

This acquisition is issued as a 100% Small Business Set-A-Side. Award will be made on a firm-fixed price basis for CLINs 0001, 0002, 0004, 0005, 0007, and 0008. CLINs 0003, 0006, and 0009 (Travel Expenses) will be awarded on a cost-reimbursable not-to-exceed basis.

The Period of Performance is as follows:

Leadership and team development services

USGS is tax exempt.

opps.description:
On behalf of Department of Commerce (DOC), GSA is conducting market research.

DO NOT submit your response in eBuy. Your response will not be included in the report that is provided to the customer. NOTE: If the link does not work, try clearing your browser history (cache) and then copying and pasting the link into a new browser. To respond to this RFI, you MUST copy and paste the below link into a new browser.

Only responses submitted through the linked RFI will be accepted. Nothing else will be evaluated or recorded (this includes selecting 'interested' within eBuy).


Link to RFI: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_3vHEA7ULWARVrOm


Additional Information:
Due to the structure of contracts in GSA eBuy, you may receive multiple Sources Sought or RFI notifications for the same requirement if it falls under multiple of your company's awarded SINs. However, please note that you are only required to respond to the RFI once.

Be sure to use the URL provided above to submit your response.


Any questions regarding this RFI will be responded to by email only.  Send your inquiry to: rfi@research.gsa.gov and be sure to include the RFQ number.
